learnings.title: Sonnet schema hallucinations
learnings.lesson: Sonnet 4.6 hallucinated table and column names when building a frontend against ITD_AggDB. Created QAMSCert (actual: QAMS), used DocType/ResultValue/EnteredBy (actual: RecTypeID/TestValue/CreatedBy). Always verify AI-generated schema references against INFORMATION_SCHEMA before trusting.
learnings.severity: HIGH — Christopher spent 15 minutes double-checking the DB because of phantom references

learnings.title: Link table PKs are not auto-increment
learnings.lesson: ITD_AggDB link tables have dual-column pattern: 'id' (identity, auto) and '*LinkID' (business PK, NOT auto). INSERT must supply explicit *LinkID via MAX+1. Applies to RecLink, StatusLink, TestResultLink, QAMSLink, MaterialTypeLink, AccessRecLink, all *Link tables.

learnings.title: Windows-MCP Shell terminal visibility
learnings.lesson: Shell commands often create terminal processes that flash and close. For pip installs, Python checks, and long operations: write a .py or .ps1 to disk first, execute it, and read output from a file. Don't fight stdout capture through nested shells.

learnings.title: Python portability on Windows
learnings.lesson: Robocopy of full Python install (with torch/cait site-packages) is unreliable across network shares. Use official embeddable zip: download, uncomment 'import site' in ._pth, bootstrap get-pip.py, install only needed deps. Clean and ~50MB vs 634MB.
learnings.severity: HIGH — botched copy wasted 20+ minutes and produced broken stdlib

learnings.title: IGS domain GPO blocks non-standard ports
learnings.lesson: Domain firewall GPO blocks inter-machine traffic on ports like 8000, 1433. Port 80 and 445 are allowed because IIS and SMB are registered services. Local firewall rules (New-NetFirewallRule) cannot override GPO. Solution: IIS reverse proxy via ARR on port 80.
learnings.severity: HIGH — completely blocked Rebecca's access until proxy

learnings.title: Long-running processes via Invoke-Command die
learnings.lesson: Processes spawned via Invoke-Command or Start-Process in remote PS sessions die when session ends (session isolation). Register-ScheduledTask with SYSTEM account + AtStartup trigger is the reliable path for services that must survive.
learnings.severity: HIGH — server kept dying between tests

learnings.title: IIS authorization section config is fragile
learnings.lesson: IIS authorization at app level: must unlock sections via appcmd first, use full path -section:system.webServer/security/authorization (not shorthand), and Web-Url-Auth feature must be installed. Web.config-based auth can conflict with ARR proxy causing 500.19. Safest: appcmd at apphost commit path.

learnings.title: IIS reverse proxy runs as service account, not user
learnings.lesson: When IIS proxies to FastAPI, the SQL connection uses the process identity (SYSTEM via scheduled task), not the authenticated IIS user. SYSTEM needs explicit SQL login with db_datareader/datawriter. User identity stays at the IIS layer only.
learnings.severity: HIGH — 503 until SYSTEM got SQL access
